entities.name: FINGER MILLET
entities.description: A dual-purpose crop grown in semi-arid environments of East Africa and South Asia to address food, forage, and nutritional needs.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environmental conditions characterized by low rainfall, where finger millet is typically grown.

entities.name: EAST AFRICA
entities.description: A region where finger millet is grown.

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: A region where finger millet is grown.

entities.name: YIELD POTENTIAL
entities.description: The maximum possible productivity of a crop under ideal conditions.

entities.name: SMALL MILLETS
entities.description: A group of millet crops that includes finger millet.

entities.name: GENETIC GAINS
entities.description: The increase in the average performance of a crop population due to breeding.

entities.name: CLIMATE RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to maintain productivity despite climate-related stresses.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop output per unit area in farmer's fields.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The scientific process of improving crop traits through selection and hybridization.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: The diversity in genetic characteristics available within a crop species for improvement.

entities.name: HYBRIDIZATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used to cross different genetic lines to create hybrids with desired traits.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: An international center focused on improving mandate crops like finger millet.

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: Genetic resources, such as seeds or tissues, used for breeding programs.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country where finger millet breeding began and is currently active.

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: A country where ICRISAT's finger millet program is based.

entities.name: INDIAN GENE POOL
entities.description: The genetic diversity of finger millet originating from India.

entities.name: AFRICAN GENE POOL
entities.description: The genetic diversity of finger millet originating from Africa.

entities.name: BLAST RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to resist blast disease.

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of the finger millet genome.

entities.name: PHENOTYPING PROTOCOLS
entities.description: Advanced methods for measuring and analyzing physical traits of a crop.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Physical characteristics of a crop related to its growth, yield, and management.

entities.name: VALUE ADDITION
entities.description: The process of increasing the economic value of a crop through processing or improvement.

entities.name: TRADE VALUE
entities.description: The market value of a crop in commercial trade.

entities.name: SOUTHEAST ASIA
entities.description: A region where improving finger millet benefits marginal farmers.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A region where improving finger millet benefits marginal farmers.
